topic: Absolute Value Inequalities and Their Applications

questions.question: The absolute value inequality $|2x + 3| leq 5$ is equivalent to:
The temperature in a greenhouse varies between $65^circ$F and $75^circ$F. If $t$ represents the temperature, which inequality best represents this situation?
The graph of the solution to $|x + 1| > 4$ is:
The city of Miami has a rainfall of $x$ inches per month. The amount of rainfall $x$ satisfies $|x - 4| leq 2$. This means that:
The absolute value inequality $|x - 5| geq 2$ is equivalent to:

A certain species of birds can only survive in temperatures between $40^circ$F and $80^circ$F. If $T$ represents the temperature in degrees Fahrenheit, write an absolute value inequality that represents the temperature range in which the birds can survive. Provide your answer in interval notation.

The water level in a lake varies between $20$ feet and $40$ feet. If $w$ represents the water level, write an absolute value inequality that represents this situation. Then, solve the inequality and provide the solution in interval notation.

teacher_rules: Tip 1: Emphasize the importance of understanding the concept of absolute value and its application in inequalities.
Tip 2: Make sure students can graph the solution sets on a number line and express them in interval notation.
Tip 3: Encourage students to use real-world examples, such as weather and environmental changes, to model absolute value inequalities.
